commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: No hover or focus states on forecast day cards. They appear clickable but provide no visual feedback when users interact with them.
issues.fix: Add hover and focus states to forecast boxes with background color change and subtle scale/shadow transitions (200ms ease). Consider making them interactive with an onClick handler prop.

issues.description: No loading or empty state handling. If forecast array is empty, no message is shown; if data is loading, there's no skeleton or spinner.
issues.fix: Add conditional rendering for empty forecast (show 'No forecast available') and accept optional loading prop to display placeholder skeleton states.

issues.description: Temperature display uses inline flex div instead of Stack primitive, breaking consistency with design system component usage.
issues.fix: Replace the inline div with <Stack direction='horizontal' align='baseline' gap='var(--ggui-spacing-1)'> for consistency.

issues.description: Forecast card padding (12px) is smaller than main card (24px), which may feel visually imbalanced.
issues.fix: Consider increasing forecast card padding to 16px for better visual hierarchy and consistency.

issues.description: No aria-label on Icon components. Screen readers won't know what weather conditions the icons represent.
issues.fix: Add aria-label or aria-description props to Icon components describing the weather condition (e.g., aria-label={condition}).

issues.description: Forecast cards lack semantic structure. They're just Boxes without role or landmark attributes to indicate they're forecast items.
issues.fix: Wrap forecast items in <article> or <section> elements with appropriate role='region' and aria-label='5-day forecast' on the container.

issues.description: Props interface doesn't include wind speed unit (e.g., km/h, mph). Component hardcodes 'km/h' as the unit.
issues.fix: Add optional windSpeedUnit prop with default 'km/h' to allow flexible unit display.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements all core weather card features with excellent design system compliance—colors, spacing, and typography all use CSS variables. The layout is clean and well-structured with good visual hierarchy. However, it lacks polish in interactive states (no hover effects on forecast cards) and empty/loading state handling, which are important for production use. The code is well-organized with proper prop destructuring and sensible defaults, though a few accessibility enhancements (aria-labels, semantic HTML for forecast) would improve screen reader support.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid data handling and good design token usage, but lacks interactive polish (no hover/focus states on forecast boxes) and could improve visual hierarchy with better heading levels. The forecast strip uses `wrap={true}` which may cause unexpected breaks on smaller screens despite `flex: 0 1 auto`.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

issues.description: Props interface does not include onSubmit callback - the requirement states 'Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers' but onSubmit is never defined in Props interface or invoked. The handleSubmit function only simulates a callback with a comment 'in real use, this would be props.onSubmit'.
issues.fix: Add onSubmit to Props interface: onSubmit: (data: FormData) => Promise<void> | void; Then invoke it in handleSubmit: await props.onSubmit(submitPayload);

issues.description: Source code appears truncated at the end - the review step render is incomplete. The comments section shows 'formData.features.join(', ') followed by backticks suggesting cut-off mid-render.
issues.fix: Provide complete source code with full review step and navigation buttons rendering.

issues.description: No Back/Next button pair is visible in the provided code. Navigation buttons (Back/Next) are defined as handler functions but never rendered. The component has handleNext and handleBack but no Button elements to trigger them.
issues.fix: Add a navigation Stack at the bottom with Back and Next buttons: <Stack gap={12} direction='horizontal'><Button onClick={handleBack} disabled={currentStep === 1}>Back</Button><Button onClick={handleNext} disabled={!isCurrentStepValid()}>Next</Button></Stack>

issues.description: Progress bar label shows 'Step 2 of 4' format but Progress component only receives value={currentStep} and max={4}. The showLabel prop may render mathematically correct labels, but custom formatting of 'Step X of 4' is not explicitly implemented.
issues.fix: Verify Progress component accepts showLabel=true and renders 'Step {currentStep} of 4' format, or add custom text above/below Progress showing current step indicator.

issues.description: Submit button on review step (Step 5) is not rendered in the provided code. The handleSubmit function exists but no UI element invokes it.
issues.fix: Add Submit button on currentStep === 5: <Button onClick={handleSubmit} disabled={submitStatus === 'submitting'} variant='primary'>Submit Survey</Button>

issues.description: RadioGroup and Checkbox components lack explicit aria-describedby linking error messages. The error prop is passed but no aria-describedby connects the error text to the form control for screen readers.
issues.fix: Ensure RadioGroup and Checkbox components internally implement aria-describedby pointing to error message IDs, or wrap in fieldset with aria-labelledby and aria-describedby.

issues.description: Input components use error prop for validation but no aria-invalid='true' is explicitly set. Screen readers may not announce invalid state properly.
issues.fix: Add aria-invalid={!!fieldErrors.name} and aria-describedby='name-error' to Input components.

issues.description: Progress indicator lacks aria-label. Screen readers will not announce what the progress bar represents (e.g., 'Survey progress, step 2 of 4').
issues.fix: Add aria-label={`Survey progress, step ${currentStep} of 4`} to Progress component.

issues.description: Props does not have default values defined. If parent component fails to pass featureOptions or satisfactionLabels, component will render empty arrays without fallback.
issues.fix: Add SurveyForm.defaultProps = { featureOptions: [], satisfactionLabels: ['Very Unsatisfied', 'Unsatisfied', 'Neutral', 'Satisfied', 'Very Satisfied'] }; or use default parameter in destructuring.

issues.description: Review step uses hardcoded fallback color in Box background: 'var(--ggui-color-surfaceVariant, #f4f4f5)'. While design tokens are used, the fallback hex value violates requirement of 'use design system CSS variables for all styling'.
issues.fix: Remove hardcoded hex fallback or ensure design system provides the variable. Use: background='var(--ggui-color-surfaceVariant)' only.

evaluations.critique: The survey form demonstrates solid structure with proper multi-step navigation, validation logic, and state management. However, critical features are missing: the onSubmit callback is never wired from props, navigation buttons are not rendered, and the source code is truncated making final output unclear. Accessibility is weak with missing aria-invalid and aria-describedby attributes on form controls. With these issues addressed—particularly implementing the props.onSubmit callback, rendering Back/Next/Submit buttons, and completing the truncated code—this would be a strong production component.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid layout structure with proper responsive container and clear step separation, but significantly underutilizes ggui design tokens by relying on hardcoded pixel values (24, 16, 12, 8) for spacing instead of var(--ggui-spacing-*). Missing design tokens for colors, weak visual hierarchy on the progress indicator, and some UI polish gaps (no disabled state styling on Next button when invalid, no transition effects between steps).

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Card elements used as clickable grid cells lack proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support. Screen readers won't announce the element and non-mouse users cannot interact with them.
issues.fix: Convert grid cells to interactive button elements with proper ARIA labels (e.g., aria-label="Hydrogen, H, atomic number 1") and keyboard event handlers (onKeyDown for Enter/Space)

issues.description: No focus visible indicators on clickable element cards. Users navigating by keyboard won't see focus state.
issues.fix: Add focus styles to the Card elements: outline or border change on :focus-visible. Consider adding a CSS class or style prop for focus state.

issues.description: Legend badges use variant names that may not be semantic. Category names in legend aren't descriptive (e.g., 'alkali-metal' without proper label formatting).
issues.fix: Format category labels with proper spacing/capitalization and consider adding aria-label to the legend section describing it as a color legend

issues.description: Element names displayed below symbols in grid cells may cause visual clutter in smaller cells. The text hierarchy between symbol and name could be clearer.
issues.fix: Consider removing element names from grid cells (only show in detail panel) or reduce their font size and opacity further. This maintains focus on symbol/atomic number.

issues.description: Hover effect (scale + shadow) is only applied on mouse enter/leave. Doesn't provide feedback for keyboard focus or touch devices.
issues.fix: Add focus state styling to match hover effect when cards receive keyboard focus, and ensure touch-friendly interaction with larger hit targets if needed

issues.description: Specification mentions 'detail panel below grid' but doesn't explicitly require the element name in grid cells. Current implementation shows name, symbol, and atomic number in all grid cells which may be redundant since detail panel shows full information.
issues.fix: Consider reducing grid cell info to only symbol and atomic number for cleaner visual presentation (element name already shown in detail panel)

evaluations.critique: The periodic table component is well-structured with excellent state management, smooth hover transitions, and proper use of design tokens for category colors. The search functionality, legend, and detail panel all work correctly. However, accessibility is the main concern: clickable elements lack semantic button structure, keyboard navigation support, ARIA labels, and visible focus indicators—critical for inclusivity. Visual design could be slightly improved by reducing clutter in grid cells and making category labels more readable."

evaluation.critique: The component correctly implements a CSS Grid periodic table with proper element positioning and responsive filtering. However, there are design token inconsistencies: hardcoded rgba() values in shadow styles, fallback hex colors that should rely on tokens, and inline style transforms that bypass the design system. The detail panel and legend are well-structured, but the overall polish could be improved with consistent token usage and more refined interactive states.

commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

example.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
example.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

addToCart.label: Add to Cart
addToCart.description: Called when user clicks Add to Cart. Payload: { productId, quantity }

product.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
product.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

reviews.author: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Great sound quality. A bit tight for my head but breaks in after a week.

reviews.author: James P.
reviews.text: Battery life is exactly as advertised. Very impressed.

issues.description: Source code is truncated/incomplete. The closing Button tag is cut off ('</Butt') and the Toast component rendering is missing entirely, despite being imported and initialized with showToast state.
issues.fix: Complete the source code. Finish the Button closing tag and add the Toast component render at the end of the JSX with proper feedback display.

issues.description: Toast component is imported and state is managed (showToast, feedback) but never rendered in the JSX, making feedback notifications non-functional.
issues.fix: Add Toast render before closing Container: <Toast open={showToast} type={feedback?.type} message={feedback?.message} onClose={() => setShowToast(false)} />

issues.description: Code is syntactically broken - the final Button element is unclosed ('<Butt' instead of '</Button>'), which will cause a compilation error.
issues.fix: Change '</Butt' to '</Button>' to properly close the element and fix JSX syntax.

issues.description: On mobile, the image/details layout uses 'horizontal' direction with a minWidth of 400px, which will cause overflow and poor responsiveness on small screens. No responsive breakpoint handling visible.
issues.fix: Implement responsive layout: use direction='vertical' on mobile widths (e.g., via a media query or conditional rendering based on viewport width). Consider using flexWrap or conditional layouts.

issues.description: Hardcoded inline styles (minWidth '400px', height '400px', flex values) for image placeholder override design system spacing consistency and don't respect responsive requirements.
issues.fix: Replace inline style objects with design system values or use responsive conditional styling. Image should scale responsively and stack on mobile.

issues.description: Tab navigation buttons likely lack proper ARIA roles/attributes. The Tabs component may not expose tablist, tab, and tabpanel roles correctly for screen readers.
issues.fix: Ensure Tabs primitive from @ggui-ai/design includes proper ARIA roles. If not, manually add role='tablist' to container and role='tab' / aria-selected to tab buttons.

issues.description: Quantity increment/decrement buttons have no aria-label. Screen reader users won't understand their purpose without labels.
issues.fix: Add aria-label='Decrease quantity' and aria-label='Increase quantity' to the decrement and increment buttons respectively.

issues.description: Price display lacks semantic meaning (just text). No label 'Price:' prefix before the amount, making it ambiguous for screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap price in a labeled container or add explicit text like 'Price: {currency} {productPrice}' for clarity.

issues.description: No visible hover/focus states on interactive elements like buttons and quantity controls. No CSS transitions defined.
issues.fix: Ensure Button and interactive elements have visible :hover and :focus-visible states. Use CSS transitions (e.g., 200ms ease) on opacity, background-color, or transform for smooth feedback.

issues.description: Disabled state visual feedback may be unclear for the 'Add to Cart' button when stock is 0 or during submission. No distinct styling change.
issues.fix: Ensure Button primitive renders disabled state with reduced opacity/saturation and cursor: not-allowed. Verify loading state is visually distinct (e.g., spinner icon).

issues.description: Toast auto-dismiss uses setTimeout (3000ms) without a visual countdown indicator or ability for user to close it before auto-dismiss.
issues.fix: Add an optional close button on the Toast or display a progress bar to indicate remaining time before auto-dismiss.

issues.description: Image placeholder color uses primary-50 which may be very light and lack sufficient contrast with white background. Text inside may be hard to read.
issues.fix: Test contrast ratio or use a more saturated design token like primary-100 or primary-200 for the placeholder background.

issues.description: Multiple hardcoded color fallbacks scattered throughout (e.g., 'var(--ggui-color-onSurface, #18181b)'). While this handles missing tokens, it pollutes the code.
issues.fix: Create a utility object mapping token names to ensure consistent fallbacks, or verify that all required tokens exist in the design system.

evaluations.critique: The component has a strong foundation with proper state management and attempted use of design tokens, but is severely hampered by incomplete/truncated source code (missing Button closing tag and Toast render). The layout responsiveness is inadequate for mobile (fixed 400px image width with horizontal direction), and accessibility features like aria-labels on buttons and proper ARIA roles on tabs are missing. While interactive features are implemented (quantity control, add-to-cart), visual polish and feedback states need strengthening.

issues.description: Image placeholder has fixed min-width of 400px which will break responsiveness on mobile widths. The prompt specifically requires 'stack image above details on mobile widths', but the current layout uses horizontal flex without breakpoints.
issues.fix: Add responsive styling using media queries or conditional rendering to switch from horizontal to vertical stack on screens below 768px. Alternatively, use CSS Grid with auto-fit or set image max-width to 100% on smaller screens.

issues.description: No visual feedback or transition when tabs are switched. Tab content appears instantly without smooth transitions.
issues.fix: Add CSS transition (e.g., opacity: 0.2s ease) or animate.css-style fade-in when activeTabKey changes to provide visual continuity between tab switches.

issues.description: Tab items lack semantic markup (e.g., <button role="tab"> or proper ARIA attributes). Tab navigation should have aria-selected, aria-controls, and role="tablist" for screen reader compatibility.
issues.fix: Ensure Tabs primitive from @ggui-ai/design includes proper ARIA semantics internally, or wrap tab triggers with appropriate roles and attributes for keyboard navigation and screen reader announcement.

issues.description: The hardcoded color values in inline styles (e.g., 'var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)') use fallback hex codes instead of purely relying on design tokens. While fallbacks are pragmatic, they technically bypass the token system.
issues.fix: Use only design token variables without fallback hex codes, or ensure all fallbacks are documented as part of the design system specification.

issues.description: Price formatting shows 'USD {price}' but currency prop value should come first based on typical currency symbol placement. For example, $ should precede the amount (e.g., '$99.99' not 'USD 99.99').
issues.fix: Add a simple currency symbol map (USD → $, EUR → €, etc.) and format as '{currencySymbol}{price}' or check if currency prop is already a symbol vs. code.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements nearly all requested features with clean, well-structured code and solid accessibility foundations. It correctly uses design tokens throughout, manages state properly, and handles the add-to-cart flow with loading/error feedback. The main gap is the lack of responsive mobile layout (image should stack above details on small screens) and missing smooth transitions between tab content. Minor improvements to tab accessibility semantics and currency formatting would bring it to production-ready quality.

evaluation.critique: The component has good visual hierarchy and mostly correct data presentation, but suffers from hardcoded inline styles (minWidth, height, flex values in px/auto) that bypass design tokens and reduce responsiveness. The color fallbacks use hardcoded hex values instead of pure token variables, and there's insufficient polish in interactive states (no visible hover effects on cards/buttons, missing focus states).

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component with real-time messaging. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge
- Typing indicator (shows when another user is typing, received via stream)

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing messages (initial from props, new ones from stream)
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call the sendMessage action with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- Initial messages from props, new messages arrive via stream — append them to the list
- Typing indicator appears/disappears via stream events
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom when new messages arrive

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Initial messages to display on mount

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

stream.description: Real-time chat events from the agent

message.description: New message from another participant — append to message list

typing.description: Typing indicator — show/hide based on active flag

loadHistory.label: Load History
loadHistory.description: Fetch older messages for infinite scroll

getDraftState.description: Current message draft and typing state

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

stream.description: Real-time chat events from the agent

message.description: New message from another participant — append to message list

typing.description: Typing indicator — show/hide based on active flag

issues.description: Send button lacks explicit hover/focus visual feedback beyond disabled state. No transition on background color change.
issues.fix: Add CSS transitions (200ms ease) to button on :hover and :focus states in variant styling, or ensure Button primitive provides this by default.

issues.description: Message bubbles have tight max-width (70%) that may wrap awkwardly on mobile or for short messages. No visual distinction between sender avatar/initials.
issues.fix: Consider increasing max-width slightly (75-80%), or add optional avatar component from design system for visual grouping. Add responsive breakpoints for narrower screens.

issues.description: Input field label is visually hidden ('Message input') but helpful for screen readers. Focus ring color on input should use design tokens (currently relies on browser default).
issues.fix: Ensure Input primitive applies proper focus states with design system tokens. Consider adding aria-describedby or aria-label if placeholder is the only visible text.

issues.description: Typing indicator uses hardcoded white text color (#ffffff) for current user messages instead of design token fallback pattern used elsewhere.
issues.fix: Change hardcoded #ffffff colors to 'var(--ggui-color-onPrimary, #ffffff)' for consistency with design system approach.

issues.description: Message grouping logic doesn't include a stable key (uses array index), which could cause reconciliation issues if messages are filtered or reordered.
issues.fix: Use `msg.timestamp` or `msg.sender + msg.timestamp` as key instead of `msgIdx` to ensure stable DOM identity.

evaluations.critique: Excellent implementation of a real-time chat interface with solid feature completeness. Header, message list, and input areas are all present and functional; stream integration for new messages and typing indicators works correctly. Message grouping by sender is well-implemented, and auto-scroll behavior is smooth. Strengths include proper use of design tokens, semantic HTML (role="log" for accessibility), and clean state management. Minor improvements: send button needs explicit hover transitions, input accessibility could be strengthened with focus styling, and hardcoded white text colors should use design tokens for consistency. Overall very close to production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid overall structure with proper flex layout and responsive scrolling, but contains hardcoded hex colors (#ffffff, #0284c7) that should use design tokens instead. The input area styles are cut off mid-line, and while interactive features like typing indicators and message grouping are well-implemented, hover/focus states on the input button and text selection styling are missing.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices with live updates. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp (updates in real-time)
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage
- Brief flash/highlight animation when a stock price updates

Initial stock data comes from props. Live price updates arrive via stream events — merge them into the displayed data. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

stream.description: Real-time stock price updates from the agent

priceUpdate.description: Updated price data for one or more stocks

getStockDetail.label: Stock Detail
getStockDetail.description: Get detailed info for a specific stock (52-week range, volume, etc.)

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The source code appears truncated at the bottom (Navigation Buttons section is incomplete). Cannot fully verify that all navigation buttons (Back/Next/Complete) are fully implemented with correct disabled states.
issues.fix: Complete the Navigation Buttons section to ensure Back button is hidden on step 1, Next is disabled when validation fails, and Complete button is properly wired.

issues.description: Avatar selection buttons lack semantic accessibility. They are clickable Box elements with no keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, or semantic role. This breaks keyboard-only user access to the avatar picker.
issues.fix: Convert avatar buttons to use proper button elements or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, aria-label={`Select avatar ${emoji}`}, and keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space).

issues.description: Step indicator text 'Step X of 3' lacks semantic context. No main landmark or skip link structure. The progress bar is visual-only with no accessible label describing its purpose.
issues.fix: Wrap main content in <main> landmark, add aria-label to Progress component (e.g., 'Progress: Step 2 of 3'), and use <h1> for page title if applicable.

issues.description: Avatar selection buttons have a transition style but no hover/focus visual feedback beyond the background color change. Button-like elements should show clear focus rings for keyboard navigation.
issues.fix: Add focus-visible pseudo-class styling with a contrast ring (outline or border), and enhance hover state with scale or shadow for better visual feedback.

issues.description: Avatar selection buttons use hardcoded colors in inline styles ('var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)' and 'var(--ggui-color-outline, #d4d4d8)') rather than relying purely on design token props. While tokens are used, the fallback values suggest inconsistent token mapping.
issues.fix: Ensure all color values are passed via props or consistently use the design system's color API without fallback hex values.

issues.description: The validateStep2 function incorrectly assigns the role validation error to 'newErrors.name' instead of 'newErrors.role'. This is a logic bug that prevents proper error messaging for the role field.
issues.fix: Change line in validateStep2 from 'newErrors.name = ...' to 'newErrors.role = ...' to correctly assign role validation errors.

issues.description: No loading state handling during onComplete callback. If the submission is asynchronous, there's no disabled state on the Complete button or loading indicator to prevent double-submissions.
issues.fix: Add isSubmitting state, disable Complete button during submission, and optionally show a loading spinner or skeleton state.

issues.description: Step transitions use key={`step-${currentStep}`} on Card, which causes re-mounting but no visible transition animation (fade or slide). The change between steps feels abrupt.
issues.fix: Add CSS transitions or use a fade-in animation (opacity: 0 to 1 with 200ms ease) when the step content mounts to smooth the UX.

evaluations.critique: The onboarding wizard is well-structured with good form state management and comprehensive validation logic. The component successfully implements all 3 steps, step indicator with progress bar, and navigation logic. However, there are several polish and accessibility gaps: avatar selection buttons lack keyboard navigation and semantic roles, the Step 2 role validation has a logic bug (assigning to `name` instead of `role`), and step transitions feel abrupt without animation. The design is clean but could benefit from enhanced focus states and smooth transitions between steps.

evaluation.critique: The component has good structural hierarchy and proper data rendering from props, but significantly violates design token usage with hardcoded hex colors and inline pixel values for spacing/sizing throughout (e.g., '#0284c7', width/height '56px', padding '12px' in style objects). The avatar selection uses inline styles instead of design tokens, and several spacing values bypass the token system.
